title: Pronouncing Plurals and Articles
learningGoal: Master the pronunciation of plural 's', irregular plurals, and the linking sounds of 'a', 'an', and 'the'.
instructions: 複数形の「s」の音や、冠詞（a, an, the）が名詞とくっつく時の「リンキング（音の繋がり）」を練習します。マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Practice the plural 's' sounds and the linking sounds when articles connect with nouns. Repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Say the 'a' very quickly and lightly, connecting it directly to 'book'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'エー ブック (Eh book)' with a heavy stress on 'A'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Link the 'n' in 'an' with the 'a' in 'apple'. It sounds like 'a-nap-ple'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Pausing between the words: 'アン、アップル'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Use your teeth for the 'th' sound. Very short 'e' sound.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'ザ (za)' like the Japanese 'za zi zu ze zo'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Before a vowel, 'the' is pronounced 'thee'. Link the 'ee' sound to 'apple': thee-yap-ple.
The plural 's' here sounds like a buzzing 'z'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'アップルス (applu-su)' with a sharp 's'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Because 'box' ends in an 's' sound, the plural is pronounced as an extra syllable '-iz'.
Link the 'n' in 'an' with the 'o' in 'orange'. It sounds like 'a-nor-ange'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Pronouncing it the same as the singular 'man' (マン).

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: This is irregular! The 'o' is pronounced like an 'i'. WI-men.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'ウーマンズ (womans)' or 'ウォーメン (wo-men)'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Chill-dren. Make sure the 'r' is not a Japanese 'ら'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'チルドレンズ (childrens)' (children is already plural, don't add s!).

expectedPronunciationText: a book an apple the car the apple apples boxes an orange men women children

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! Your linking sounds (an apple -> a-nap-ple) are very natural.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review the linking rules. Don't pause between 'an' and the noun.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember that 'women' is pronounced 'WI-men', not 'WO-men'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: a book an apple the car the apple apples boxes an orange men women children
acceptedVariants: a book an apple the car the apple apples boxes an orange men women children
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: a book an apple the car the apple apples boxes an orange men women children
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: a buk an apl the kar thee apl aplz boksiz an orenj men wimen children

audioAssets.audioTranscript: a book, an apple, the car, the apple, apples, boxes, an orange, men, women, children

hintText: To sound like a native speaker, don't pause between the article and the noun. 'An apple' should sound like one word.
